parts.answer: The only correct answer is C (the oxide ion is not very polarisable) A is incorrect because the magnesium ion is polarising B is incorrect because magnesium oxide is almost perfectly ionic D is incorrect because the ions will be almost perfectly spherical (1) Number Answer Mark

The only correct answer is A (∆ hyd H(Cl − ) = ∆ sol H(NaCl) − ∆ hyd H(Na + ) + ∆ LE H(NaCl)) B is incorrect because ∆ sol H = Σ∆ hyd − ∆ LE H so ∆ hyd H(Na + ) should be subtracted not added C is incorrect because ∆ sol H = Σ∆ hyd − ∆ LE H so ∆ sol H(NaCl) and ∆ LE H(NaCl) should be added not subtracted D is incorrect because ∆ sol H = Σ∆ hyd − ∆ LE H so ∆ sol H(NaCl) should be added not subtracted (1) Number Answer Mark

The only correct answer is D (the reaction has a positive entropy change of the system) A is incorrect because activation energy does not affect thermodynamic feasibility B is incorrect because enthalpy changes of hydration of ion are always negative C is incorrect because the enthalpy change is positive so the entropy change of the surrounding will be negative (1) Number Answer Mark A is incorrect because this is the concentration of the hydrogen ions not the pH B is incorrect because this is the value for log [H + ] not −log [H + ] D is incorrect because this is minus the concentration of hydrogen ions not −log [H + ] (1) Number Answer Mark A is incorrect because this is the value of pOH B is incorrect because this value for pH assumes 0.2 mol barium hydroxide produces 0.1 mol hydroxide ions C is incorrect because this value for pH assumes 0.2 mol barium hydroxide produces 0.2 mol hydroxide ions (1) Number Answer Mark A is incorrect because this is the pH of the original hydrochloric acid B is incorrect because this is the pH of the hydrochloric acid diluted with 20 cm of water D is incorrect because this assumes equal volumes of the same concentration were mixed (1) Number Answer Mark A is incorrect because this is the value for pH of the ethanoic acid before the sodium hydroxide is added B is incorrect because this is the value for pH if 20 cm of water were added, not sodium hydroxide D is incorrect because this is the value obtained from multiplying the number of moles of acid in excess by K a and then using this answer without square rooting for the pH calculation (1) Number Answer Mark N 2 because they have little steric hindrance) A is incorrect because primary halogenoalkanes react by S N B is incorrect because primary halogenoalkanes • calculation of the number of moles • calculation of mole fraction • calculation of partial pressure • calculation of K p and justification (1) (1) (1) (1) Example of calculation SO SO O 1.20 2.00 – 1.20 = 0.80 (mol) 1.00 – 0.60 = 0.40 (mol) 1.20 2.40 = 0.500 0.80 2.40 = 0.333 0.40 2.40 = 0.167 0.500 × 3.00 = 1.50 0.333 × 3 = 1.00 0.167 × 3.0 = 0.500 = 1.50 1.00 × 0.50 = 4.5 (atm −1 ) which is not equal to 35.2 (atm −1 ) (so not at equilibrium) (4) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark (decreasing value of K p means) • the partial pressure of products decreases • the partial pressure of reactants increases • so the equilibrium moves to the left (at higher temperature) (1) (1) (1) Allow / SO decreases Allow the numerator decreases Allow SO and O rises Allow the denominator increases (2) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • ∆ r H is negative as (as increasing temperature moves the equilibrium to the left) Either • so ∆S surroundings is positive / becomes less positive as T increases • ∆S total has decreased / become less positive Or • ∆S total = R lnK • so as K has decreased lnK / R lnK / ∆S total has decreased / becomes less positive (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) Allow reaction is exothermic Do not award entropy change is negative (3) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark An answer that makes reference to the following point: • equation for K c K c = [CO ] Ignore (g) but penalise incorrect state symbols Do not award use of rounded brackets (1) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • calculates K c • units (1) (1) Example of calculation = 1.48 × (8.2053 × 10 −2 × 450) −1 = 0.040082 / 4.0082 × 10 −2 / 4.008 × 10 −2 / 4.01× 10 −2 mol dm −3 Ignore SF (2) (Total for Question 14 = 20 marks) TOTAL FOR SECTION C = 20 MARKS TOTAL FOR PAPER = 90 MARKS
